guidelines.title: Antibiotic Dosing Guidelines for Common Infections
guidelines.content:
ANTIBIOTIC DOSING GUIDELINES

PENICILLINS:

Amoxicillin:
- Standard: 500 mg TID or 875 mg BID
- High-dose (pneumonia, sinusitis): 1000 mg TID or 875 mg TID
- Strep pharyngitis: 500 mg BID x10 days or 1000 mg daily x10 days
- Pediatric: 25-50 mg/kg/day divided TID (up to 80-90 mg/kg/day high-dose)

Amoxicillin-Clavulanate (Augmentin):
- Standard: 875/125 mg BID or 500/125 mg TID
- High-dose: 2000/125 mg BID (XR)
- Bite wounds: 875/125 mg BID x5-7 days
- Pediatric: 25-45 mg/kg/day (amox component) divided BID

Piperacillin-Tazobactam (Zosyn):
- Standard: 3.375 g IV q6h
- Serious infections: 4.5 g IV q6h
- Extended infusion: 3.375 g over 4 hours q8h (improves outcomes)
- Renal: CrCl 20-40: 2.25 g q6h; CrCl <20: 2.25 g q8h

Ampicillin-Sulbactam (Unasyn):
- 3 g IV q6h (1.5 g for less severe)
- Renal: CrCl 15-29: q12h; CrCl <15: q24h

CEPHALOSPORINS:

Cephalexin (Keflex):
- 500 mg QID or 1000 mg BID
- Skin/soft tissue: 500 mg QID x7-10 days
- Pediatric: 25-50 mg/kg/day divided QID

Cefuroxime (Ceftin):
- 250-500 mg BID
- Pneumonia: 500 mg BID x7-10 days

Ceftriaxone (Rocephin):
- 1-2 g IV/IM daily (can divide BID for CNS infections)
- Meningitis: 2 g IV q12h
- Gonorrhea: 500 mg IM x1 (now single-dose ceftriaxone only)
- Pediatric: 50-100 mg/kg/day (max 4 g/day)

Cefepime (Maxipime):
- 1-2 g IV q8-12h
- Febrile neutropenia: 2 g IV q8h
- Renal: CrCl 30-60: q12h; CrCl 11-29: q24h

Ceftazidime (Fortaz):
- 1-2 g IV q8h
- Pseudomonas coverage
- Renal: CrCl 31-50: q12h; CrCl 16-30: q24h

FLUOROQUINOLONES:

Ciprofloxacin (Cipro):
- UTI: 250-500 mg BID x3-7 days
- Complicated UTI/Pyelonephritis: 500 mg BID x7-14 days
- Respiratory (not first-line): 750 mg BID
- IV: 400 mg q8-12h

Levofloxacin (Levaquin):
- 500-750 mg daily
- CAP: 750 mg daily x5 days
- UTI: 250-500 mg daily x3 days (uncomplicated)
- Renal: CrCl 20-49: 500 mg q24h; CrCl <20: 500 mg x1, then 250 mg q24h

Moxifloxacin (Avelox):
- 400 mg daily
- CAP: 400 mg daily x5-7 days
- NO renal adjustment needed
- QTc prolongation risk

MACROLIDES:

Azithromycin (Z-pack):
- Standard: 500 mg day 1, then 250 mg days 2-5
- Alternative: 500 mg daily x3 days
- CAP: 500 mg x1, then 250 mg daily x4 days
- Chlamydia: 1 g single dose
- Mycobacterium avium: 500-600 mg daily + ethambutol

Clarithromycin (Biaxin):
- 500 mg BID x7-14 days
- H. pylori: 500 mg BID (with PPI + amoxicillin)
- XL: 1000 mg daily

OTHER ANTIBIOTICS:

Doxycycline:
- 100 mg BID
- Chlamydia: 100 mg BID x7 days
- Lyme disease: 100 mg BID x10-21 days
- MRSA skin infections: 100 mg BID x5-10 days
- Acne: 50-100 mg daily

Trimethoprim-Sulfamethoxazole (Bactrim):
- DS (160/800): 1 tablet BID
- UTI: 1 DS BID x3 days (uncomplicated)
- MRSA skin: 1-2 DS BID x5-10 days
- PCP prophylaxis: 1 DS daily or 1 SS daily
- PCP treatment: 15-20 mg/kg/day TMP component divided q6-8h

Vancomycin IV:
- Loading: 25-30 mg/kg
- Maintenance: 15-20 mg/kg q8-12h
- Target trough: 15-20 mcg/mL for serious infections
- AUC-guided dosing: Target AUC 400-600
- Renal: Adjust based on levels, may need q24-48h

Vancomycin Oral (C. diff):
- 125 mg QID x10-14 days
- Severe: 500 mg QID
- Recurrent: Fidaxomicin preferred, or tapered/pulsed vanco

Metronidazole (Flagyl):
- 500 mg TID or 250 mg QID
- C. diff (non-severe): 500 mg TID x10-14 days
- Bacterial vaginosis: 500 mg BID x7 days or 0.75% gel
- IV: 500 mg q6-8h
- Avoid alcohol (disulfiram reaction)

Clindamycin:
- 300-450 mg QID
- Serious infections IV: 600-900 mg q8h
- MRSA skin: 300-450 mg TID x5-10 days
- Toxin-producing infections (add to beta-lactam)

Linezolid (Zyvox):
- 600 mg BID (PO or IV)
- Duration typically 10-14 days
- Monitor weekly CBC (thrombocytopenia)
- Serotonin syndrome with SSRIs
- MAOI activity (tyramine diet restrictions for prolonged use)

Daptomycin (Cubicin):
- Skin/soft tissue: 4 mg/kg IV daily
- Bacteremia/endocarditis: 6-10 mg/kg IV daily
- NOT for pneumonia (inactivated by surfactant)
- Monitor CPK weekly

EVIDENCE LEVEL: Level I (IDSA guidelines)

guidelines.title: Antiviral Medication Dosing Guidelines
guidelines.content:
ANTIVIRAL MEDICATION DOSING GUIDELINES

HERPES SIMPLEX VIRUS (HSV):

Acyclovir:
- Genital HSV (primary): 400 mg TID x7-10 days OR 200 mg 5x/day
- Genital HSV (recurrent): 800 mg TID x2 days OR 400 mg TID x5 days
- Suppressive: 400 mg BID
- HSV encephalitis: 10 mg/kg IV q8h x14-21 days
- Neonatal HSV: 20 mg/kg IV q8h x14-21 days
- Herpes zoster: 800 mg 5x/day x7-10 days

Valacyclovir (Valtrex):
- Genital HSV (primary): 1000 mg BID x7-10 days
- Genital HSV (recurrent): 500 mg BID x3 days OR 1000 mg daily x5 days
- Suppressive: 500 mg daily (or 1000 mg daily if >10 outbreaks/year)
- Herpes zoster: 1000 mg TID x7 days
- Cold sores: 2000 mg BID x1 day

Famciclovir (Famvir):
- Genital HSV (primary): 250 mg TID x7-10 days
- Genital HSV (recurrent): 1000 mg BID x1 day
- Suppressive: 250 mg BID
- Herpes zoster: 500 mg TID x7 days

INFLUENZA:

Oseltamivir (Tamiflu):
- Treatment: 75 mg BID x5 days
- Prophylaxis: 75 mg daily x7-10 days (post-exposure)
- Pediatric dosing by weight:
  - ≤15 kg: 30 mg BID
  - >15-23 kg: 45 mg BID
  - >23-40 kg: 60 mg BID
  - >40 kg: 75 mg BID
- Renal: CrCl 30-60: 30 mg BID; CrCl 10-30: 30 mg daily

Zanamivir (Relenza):
- Treatment: 10 mg (2 inhalations) BID x5 days
- Prophylaxis: 10 mg (2 inhalations) daily x7-10 days
- Avoid in asthma/COPD (bronchospasm risk)

Baloxavir (Xofluza):
- 40-80 kg: 40 mg single dose
- ≥80 kg: 80 mg single dose
- Take within 48 hours of symptom onset
- Single dose treatment

HEPATITIS B:

Entecavir (Baraclude):
- Treatment-naive: 0.5 mg daily
- Lamivudine-refractory: 1 mg daily
- Renal: CrCl 30-49: 0.25 mg daily; CrCl 10-29: 0.15 mg daily

Tenofovir disoproxil (Viread):
- 300 mg daily
- Renal: CrCl 30-49: 300 mg q48h; CrCl 10-29: 300 mg q72-96h

Tenofovir alafenamide (Vemlidy):
- 25 mg daily with food
- Better renal and bone safety than TDF

HEPATITIS C (DAAs - Direct Acting Antivirals):

Sofosbuvir/Velpatasvir (Epclusa):
- 1 tablet (400/100 mg) daily x12 weeks
- Pan-genotypic
- Check for cirrhosis, prior treatment

Glecaprevir/Pibrentasvir (Mavyret):
- 3 tablets (300/120 mg total) daily x8-12 weeks
- Treatment-naive without cirrhosis: 8 weeks
- Pan-genotypic

Sofosbuvir/Ledipasvir (Harvoni):
- 1 tablet (400/90 mg) daily x8-12 weeks
- Genotype 1, 4, 5, 6
- 8 weeks if HCV RNA <6 million, no cirrhosis

HIV (Sample regimens):

Biktarvy (BIC/TAF/FTC):
- 1 tablet daily
- CrCl >30 mL/min
- First-line recommended regimen

Dovato (DTG/3TC):
- 1 tablet daily
- Two-drug regimen option
- Not if HBV co-infected

Triumfeq (DTG/ABC/3TC):
- 1 tablet daily
- Check HLA-B*5701 before ABC

CYTOMEGALOVIRUS (CMV):

Valganciclovir (Valcyte):
- Induction: 900 mg BID x21 days
- Maintenance: 900 mg daily
- Prophylaxis (transplant): 900 mg daily
- Renal adjustment required

Ganciclovir IV:
- Induction: 5 mg/kg q12h x14-21 days
- Maintenance: 5 mg/kg daily
- Renal: CrCl 50-69: 2.5 mg/kg q12h; CrCl 25-49: 2.5 mg/kg q24h

COVID-19:

Paxlovid (nirmatrelvir/ritonavir):
- 300/100 mg (2+1 tablets) BID x5 days
- Start within 5 days of symptoms
- eGFR 30-59: 150/100 mg BID
- eGFR <30: Not recommended
- Many drug interactions (ritonavir)

Remdesivir (Veklury):
- 200 mg IV day 1, then 100 mg IV daily
- Hospitalized: 5 days (10 days if ventilated)
- Outpatient high-risk: 3 days

Molnupiravir (Lagevrio):
- 800 mg (4 capsules) BID x5 days
- If Paxlovid contraindicated
- Not first-line

EVIDENCE LEVEL: Level I (IDSA/AASLD/DHHS guidelines)

guidelines.title: Antifungal Medication Dosing Guidelines
guidelines.content:
ANTIFUNGAL MEDICATION DOSING GUIDELINES

AZOLES:

Fluconazole (Diflucan):
- Vaginal candidiasis: 150 mg single dose
- Oropharyngeal candidiasis: 200 mg day 1, then 100-200 mg daily x7-14 days
- Esophageal candidiasis: 200-400 mg daily x14-21 days
- Candidemia: Loading 800 mg, then 400 mg daily
- Cryptococcal maintenance: 200-400 mg daily
- Prophylaxis (transplant): 400 mg daily
- Renal: CrCl <50: 50% dose reduction

Itraconazole (Sporanox):
- Capsules: 200 mg BID x3 days (loading), then 200 mg daily-BID
- Take with food (acidic beverage enhances absorption)
- Onychomycosis: 200 mg daily x12 weeks (toenails)
- Aspergillosis (alternative): 200 mg BID-TID
- Many drug interactions (CYP3A4)

Voriconazole (Vfend):
- Loading: 6 mg/kg IV q12h x2 doses
- Maintenance: 4 mg/kg IV q12h OR 200-300 mg PO q12h
- Target trough: 2-5 mcg/mL
- Take 1 hour before or after meals
- Photosensitivity - strict sun protection
- Visual disturbances common initially
- Many drug interactions, TDM essential

Posaconazole (Noxafil):
- Delayed-release tablets: 300 mg BID day 1, then 300 mg daily
- Oral suspension (older): 200 mg TID with high-fat meal
- IV: Same as tablets
- Prophylaxis: 300 mg daily (after loading)
- No renal adjustment for PO; avoid IV if CrCl <50 (cyclodextrin)

Isavuconazonium (Cresemba):
- Loading: 372 mg (=200 mg isavuconazole) q8h x6 doses
- Maintenance: 372 mg daily
- IV or PO (same dose)
- Fewer drug interactions than vori/posa
- Shortens QTc (contraindicated in short QT syndrome)

ECHINOCANDINS:

Caspofungin (Cancidas):
- Loading: 70 mg IV day 1
- Maintenance: 50 mg IV daily
- Hepatic impairment (Child-Pugh B): 35 mg daily
- Candidemia: Continue 14 days after last positive culture
- Invasive aspergillosis (salvage)

Micafungin (Mycamine):
- Candidemia: 100 mg IV daily
- Esophageal candidiasis: 150 mg IV daily
- Prophylaxis (transplant): 50 mg IV daily
- No hepatic/renal adjustment needed

Anidulafungin (Eraxis):
- Loading: 200 mg IV day 1
- Maintenance: 100 mg IV daily
- No hepatic/renal adjustment
- Preferred in liver disease

AMPHOTERICIN FORMULATIONS:

Amphotericin B lipid complex (Abelcet):
- 5 mg/kg IV daily
- Infuse over 2 hours

Liposomal amphotericin (AmBisome):
- 3-5 mg/kg IV daily
- Cryptococcal meningitis induction: 4-6 mg/kg
- Mucormycosis: 5-10 mg/kg

Amphotericin B deoxycholate (conventional):
- 0.5-1 mg/kg IV daily
- Test dose: 1 mg over 20-30 min
- Significant nephrotoxicity - largely replaced by lipid formulations
- Premedicate: Acetaminophen, diphenhydramine, ± meperidine

OTHER ANTIFUNGALS:

Flucytosine (5-FC):
- 25 mg/kg QID (100 mg/kg/day)
- Always use in combination (resistance develops quickly)
- Cryptococcal meningitis: With amphotericin
- Target level: Peak 50-100 mcg/mL, trough <25
- Renal: CrCl 20-40: q12h; CrCl 10-20: q24h
- Monitor CBC (bone marrow suppression)

Terbinafine (Lamisil):
- Onychomycosis: 250 mg daily x6 weeks (fingernails), 12 weeks (toenails)
- Tinea: 250 mg daily x2-6 weeks
- Pulse dosing (off-label): 500 mg daily x1 week/month x3-4 months
- LFT monitoring for prolonged use

Nystatin:
- Oral suspension: 400,000-600,000 units QID (swish and swallow)
- Topical: Apply 2-3x daily
- Not absorbed systemically

SPECIFIC INFECTION PROTOCOLS:

Candidemia:
- Echinocandin preferred (micafungin, caspofungin, anidulafungin)
- Fluconazole if susceptible and clinically stable
- Remove central lines
- Duration: 14 days after first negative culture
- Ophthalmology consult

Invasive Aspergillosis:
- First-line: Voriconazole or isavuconazole
- Alternative: Liposomal amphotericin
- Duration: Minimum 6-12 weeks
- Continue until immunosuppression resolves

Cryptococcal Meningitis:
- Induction: Liposomal amphotericin 3-4 mg/kg + flucytosine x2 weeks
- Consolidation: Fluconazole 400-800 mg daily x8 weeks
- Maintenance: Fluconazole 200 mg daily x1 year minimum

EVIDENCE LEVEL: Level I (IDSA Candidiasis, Aspergillosis guidelines)
